title: Customized Login Page Experience After OTP Email Verification
story: As a DataTools Pro user who just verified my email, I want to see a personalized welcome screen on the login page instead of the default sign-up prompts, so that I understand the next step is to log in with Salesforce.
description:
After a new user verifies their email via OTP, they are redirected to the login page. The current login page shows generic content including a "New to DataTools Pro?" prompt which confuses verified users. The experience needs to be tailored for post-verification.

## Changes
1. **Remove confusion**: Hide the "New DataTools Pro?" section for verified users
2. **Right pane content**: Replace the default promotional content with a welcome message:
   - Heading: "Welcome!"
   - Subheading: "Login with Salesforce to start using DataTools Pro."
   - Body: "Thank you for signing up and verifying your email. DataTools Pro is a Salesforce connected app. Login to your Salesforce org to start using DataTools Pro."
3. **Custom imagery**: Use a welcome-specific background image and video

## Technical Approach
Instead of adding a URL query parameter (`status=verified`), the existing session stamp from OTP verification is reused to detect the post-verification state and conditionally render the welcome content.
acceptance_criteria: After OTP email verification, user sees a customized welcome login page
The 'New DataTools Pro?' section is hidden for verified users
Right pane displays 'Welcome!' heading with Salesforce login instructions
Welcome message includes guidance about DataTools Pro being a Salesforce connected app
Post-verification state detected via existing session stamp, not URL parameters
Default login page remains unchanged for non-verified visitors
ux_changes: Post-OTP-verification login page shows a customized welcome screen: removes confusing 'New DataTools Pro?' prompt, displays welcome heading and Salesforce login instructions, uses a welcome-specific background image.
middleware_changes: Reuses existing OTP verification session stamp to conditionally render the welcome variant of the login page instead of the default layout.
implementation_notes: Leveraged the existing session variable stamped during OTP verification flow rather than adding a URL parameter. This avoids exposing verification status in the URL. A follow-up ticket was created for custom welcome background image and video.
comment_summary: Developer noted that an existing session variable from OTP verification could replace the proposed URL parameter approach. Implemented and deployed directly to production. Product owner praised the result and created a follow-up ticket for custom background image and video for the welcome screen.
